These figures are the sole empirical substrate for the headline claim; any misalignment between results and claims is load-bearing.",

rationale.R1.1: Section carries two competing contribution claims ('the suite is the central contribution' vs. 'the principal methodological contribution is the tube-MPC dominance finding'); reconstructing which thesis actually governs the section is necessary before anything else can be evaluated.
rationale.R1.2: Several inferential steps need checking: (a) the assertion that 'prior ad-hoc benchmarks have failed to reveal' the margin is an empirical claim offered without evidence; (b) the scoring rubric's (1−v)^2 weighting is presented as penalizing boundary-cutting but the logical link to the claimed property is non-trivial and unexplained.
rationale.R1.3: The section's credibility rests entirely on specific reported numbers (0.81±0.03, 0.62±0.08, 0.47±0.11; 'one to two orders of magnitude' violation-rate gap on tasks 7–10). These figures are the sole empirical substrate for the headline claim; any misalignment between results and claims is load-bearing.
rationale.R1.4: Two novelty claims are prominent: (a) SG-Constraint 'remedies the lack of standardized comparison' — requires checking whether comparable suites already exist; (b) the tube-MPC dominance result is framed as a new finding obscured by prior benchmarks — requires checking delta against recent safe-RL literature.
rationale.R1.7: Park and Wei (2024) is the sole citation supporting the 'learned disturbance set' construction, which underpins the main controller-design claim; accuracy and completeness of this attribution is central. Additionally, 'constraint formulations drawn from the standard MPC literature' is uncited.

parsed.clarification_question: Is this review for external submission (where novelty against prior benchmarks and related work — R1.4 — is the primary load-bearing concern) or an internal draft review (where internal logical and evidential consistency — R1.2, R1.3 — takes precedence over novelty positioning)?
